---
title: "Desperate Housewives"
author: "Zuzanna Herniczek"
format: html
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r setup}
library(ggplot2)
library(knitr)

season  <- 1:8
viewers <- c(23.7, 22.2, 17.8, 16.0, 15.1, 13.5, 12.0, 11.2)
change  <- c(NA, diff(viewers))
data    <- data.frame(season, viewers, change)
```

## About the Show

Desperate Housewives is an American television drama series created by Marc Cherry that aired on ABC from October 3, 2004 to May 13, 2012. The show follows the lives of a group of women living on the suburban Wisteria Lane, narrated from beyond the grave by their neighbour Mary Alice Young.

The series blends mystery, dark comedy, and domestic drama, and was praised for its sharp writing and ensemble cast including Teri Hatcher, Felicity Huffman, Marcia Cross, Eva Longoria, and Nicollette Sheridan. It won numerous awards, including a Golden Globe for Best Television Series.

## Show Image

![Desperate Housewives](desperate_housewives.jpg){width=60% fig-align="center"}

## Viewership Data

The table below shows the average number of viewers (in millions) per season, along with the season-to-season change.

```{r table}
display <- data.frame(
  Season = data$season,
  `Avg. Viewers (millions)` = data$viewers,
  `Change from Previous Season` = ifelse(
    is.na(data$change), "â€”",
    paste0(ifelse(data$change > 0, "+", ""), round(data$change, 1), "M")
  ),
  check.names = FALSE
)

kable(display, align = c("c", "c", "c"))
```

**Key statistics:**

- Peak viewership: `r max(data$viewers)`M (Season `r data$season[which.max(data$viewers)]`)
- Final viewership: `r min(data$viewers)`M (Season `r data$season[which.min(data$viewers)]`)
- Total decline: `r round(data$viewers[1] - data$viewers[8], 1)`M viewers over 8 seasons

## Viewership Over Time

```{r plot-viewers}
ggplot(data, aes(x = season, y = viewers)) +
  geom_line(color = "#8B1A1A", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(color = "#8B1A1A", size = 3.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(viewers, "M")),
            vjust = -1, size = 3.2, color = "grey30") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:8) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(10, 26)) +
  labs(
    title = "Average Viewership per Season",
    subtitle = "Desperate Housewives (2004â€“2012)",
    x = "Season",
    y = "Average Viewers (millions)",
    caption = "Source: Wikipedia"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "grey40"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
```

## Season-to-Season Change in Viewership

```{r plot-change}
ggplot(data[-1, ], aes(x = season, y = change, fill = change > 0)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(change, 1), "M"),
                vjust = ifelse(change > 0, -0.5, 1.3)),
            size = 3.2, color = "grey20") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 2:8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#8B1A1A", "#2E8B57")) +
  labs(
    title = "Season-to-Season Change in Viewership",
    subtitle = "Desperate Housewives (2004â€“2012)",
    x = "Season",
    y = "Change in Viewers (millions)",
    caption = "Source: Wikipedia"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "grey40"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50")
```

## Interpretation

Desperate Housewives drew a massive audience at launch, peaking at `r data$viewers[1]`M viewers in Season 1. Ratings then declined steadily across all eight seasons, ending at `r data$viewers[8]`M by the finale.

The sharpest single-season drop came between Seasons 2 and 3, where viewership fell by `r abs(round(data$change[3], 1))`M â€” likely reflecting increased competition and a shift in the show's tone. A smaller but consistent decline of roughly `r abs(round(mean(data$change[-1]), 1))`M per season followed through to the end.

Despite the gradual audience decline, Desperate Housewives remained a top-rated show for ABC throughout its run and concluded its story deliberately in 2012 after 8 seasons and 180 episodes.
